comments.en: Ontology for wind energy benchmarking challenges, training, and interdisciplinary research. Extends schema.org, DDI lifecycle, SKOS, and PROV-O.

comment.en: A curated sequence of tutorials and challenges designed to develop specific competencies in wind energy digitalisation.
Type of component failure in wind turbines (e.g., gearbox bearing damage, generator winding fault, blade crack, pitch system malfunction).
Metric used to evaluate and compare submissions (e.g., RMSE, MAE, F1-score, AUC-ROC).
A measurable quantity from SCADA sensors, survey responses, or experimental measurements used in wind energy research.
Questionnaire or assessment tool for social science research in wind energy, including community acceptance studies, safety culture assessments, and workforce competency evaluations.
Controlled vocabulary for categorizing computational methods (e.g., deep learning, statistical, physics-based, hybrid).
Training material for wind energy digitalisation skills, including Jupyter notebooks, video lectures, documentation, and hands-on exercises.
A collection of wind turbines at a specific geographic location, sharing infrastructure and grid connection.
A response variable from surveys or questionnaires (e.g., attitude scores, demographic data, safety perception ratings).
A benchmarking competition in wind energy, consisting of one or more tasks.
A physical measurement from turbine sensors (e.g., wind speed, rotor RPM, gearbox temperature, power output, vibration amplitude).
Algorithm or methodological approach implemented in a submission.
A specific task within a challenge (e.g., failure detection, power forecasting, remaining useful life estimation).
A reference from one scholarly resource to another, capturing citation relationships with provenance information.
A participant's solution submission to a challenge task, including code, predictions, and documentation.
A ranked result entry on a challenge leaderboard, linking a submission to its performance scores.
Provenance metadata about a reference, including confidence scores, linking method, and source information for automated or manual citations.
Skill area in wind energy digitalisation (e.g., SCADA data analysis, machine learning for prognosis, time series forecasting, data governance, uncertainty quantification).
A wind energy turbine with specifications such as manufacturer, rated power, rotor diameter, and location.
Links a challenge task to the dataset officially provided for that task.
The organization responsible for hosting and managing a challenge.
Internal study or project number.
Links a resource to scholarly articles describing or using it.
The actual numerical value of a metric for a leaderboard entry.
Links a challenge to entries on its leaderboard.
Links a challenge to its constituent tasks.
Unit: individual, household, turbine, farm, blade, component.
Links a training pathway to a tutorial that is part of the curriculum.
Open Researcher and Contributor ID.
Timestamp of a sensor measurement.
Links a turbine to the wind farm where it is installed.
Links a submission to datasets actually used in model development, including external or augmented data sources.
Links a creative work to a reference describing its relationship to another resource.
Category of computational method (deprecated: use hasMethodType with MethodType class instead).
Links a submission to the computational method it implements.
Pipeline or tool that generated the reference.
Links a training pathway to a challenge that serves as practical assessment.
Theoretical constructs measured by a survey instrument.
URL to trained model files or serialized artifacts.
The person or organization responsible for a submission.
Links a leaderboard entry to the submission it represents.
Links a reference to its associated provenance metadata.
Links a tutorial to accompanying code repository.
Links a challenge task to recommended training materials.
Indicates the competency a tutorial aims to teach or develop.
Difficulty classification: beginner, intermediate, or advanced.
URL of the resource in its native web portal.
Number of turbines in a wind farm.
Links a computational method to a controlled vocabulary term describing its methodological category.
Short name or code for a variable.
Date when a survey response was collected.
Links a survey instrument to datasets where it has been applied.
Estimated duration to complete a tutorial (ISO 8601 format).
URL of source code repository (e.g., GitHub, GitLab).
Links a submission to the specific challenge task it addresses.
Start date of a challenge.
Temporal resolution of time-series data (e.g., 1Hz, 10min, 1day).
Numerical score achieved by a submission on a specific metric.
Inverse property linking a person or organization (agent) to their submissions.
Confidence score for automatically generated references (0.0 to 1.0).
Links a challenge task to the skill area it develops or assesses.
Direct URL for downloading a dataset.
Links a leaderboard entry to the challenge in which the ranking is defined.
Digital Object Identifier.
Type: cross-sectional, panel, time-series, sensor, categorical, continuous.
URI pointing to license terms.
Links a challenge task to specific turbine instances or types used in the task.
Inverse property linking a task back to its parent challenge.
Indicates that one tutorial must be completed before another.
Links a submission to the metrics used for its evaluation.
Links a resource to variables it uses or describes.
Specifies the type of turbine failure a task aims to detect or predict.
Links a resource to related datasets.
Text snippet explaining the reference context.
End date of a challenge.
Rank position on a leaderboard (1 = highest).
Human-readable description of a variable.
Method used: manual, automatic, or search-based.
Persons or organizations that participated in a challenge.
Specifies which evaluation metric a leaderboard entry value corresponds to.
Inverse property linking a method to submissions that use it.
